mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), indicates a robust and expanding economy, which typically boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 20-day returns are predominantly negative over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises significantly above the central bank's implicit target, it signals potential aggressive monetary tightening via higher FEDFUNDS, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently rises above 4.0% annualized but S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive or flat in more than 70% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand and spending power, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 gains.
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneously strong RSAFS growth and low UNRATE consistently lead to negative S&P 500 20-day returns over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the cost of capital for corporations, leading to lower earnings multiples and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive during more than 50% of identified monetary tightening cycles, as defined by the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals a weakening in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, often preceding broader economic slowdowns and negatively impacting S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10.0% over 3 months but S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive in the majority of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, which supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 5-day returns are predominantly negative over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and retail sales (RSAFS) are growing strongly, it indicates an environment of accommodative monetary policy supporting robust consumer spending, which is highly favorable for equity markets.
failure_condition: If periods with low FEDFUNDS and strong RSAFS growth do not result in positive S&P 500 20-day returns more than 65% of the time over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with slowing job growth (PAYEMS) signals an overheating labor market and potential wage inflation, which can compress profit margins and lead to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If this specific combination of low UNRATE and slowing PAYEMS growth does not precede negative S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 60% of observed instances over a 5-year period.
